column13: Go ahead with Brexit on October 31st even if it means leaving with ‘no deal’
column14: Delay Brexit until we can negotiate a deal that can pass the House of Commons
column15: Cancel Brexit and decide to remain in the European Union after all

column10: Q:V304. Boris Johnson has asked the Queen to call a new session of parliament, which requires “proroguing” parliament. Parliament was already due to be on holiday during conference season in late September, but this would extent the time that Parliament isn’t sitting and now all unfinished legislation before the prorogation will fail. Opposition MPs oppose the prorogation because they want to maximise the time available to stop a no-deal Brexit. On the other hand, the Government wants to reduce the options available to MPs to prevent any further delays to Brexit. To what extent do you support or oppose the planned prorogation of Parliament?
